kills: Pipeline logic bug: C1 Part A requires EVERY recurrent SCC of T(F, alpha, c) to have period >= 2, but the experiment's decision rule and C2's language promote a cell on ANY imprimitive SCC. A cell with one period-2 SCC and one period-1 SCC does not trigger the criterion (the measurable walk can live in the period-1 SCC). The sweep as written promotes unsound candidates.

C2(a)-to-C1 transfer is broken: lineage slicings live at rational angles 2 pi/m, and the cross-edge set {(u,v): |u - R_alpha(v)| = 1} is an exact algebraic condition in alpha, so moving alpha to an irrational value generically deletes cross edges and changes the transfer digraph (usually toward MORE connectivity, i.e. primitivity). 'An imprimitive lineage slice is a jackpot for C1' is false as stated. Only families with alpha-stable cross structure (cocircular carrier, cos alpha = 1 - 1/(2 rho^2), rho generic in an interval) can feed Part A, and the script never states or verifies that stability requirement.

Feasibility overclaim: the transfer digraph is defined on ALL proper 5-colorings of F; for a 30-60 vertex lineage sector this is astronomically larger than the claimed 10^3-10^5 blocking iterations. The criterion and the script must be restated on the interface-projected digraph (verified sound: interfaces separate consecutive cells, walks lift cell-by-cell, and the measurable-section argument runs on the projection), otherwise family (ii) and rich cells in family (iii) are unrunnable on this host.

'SHC asks for strictly less than the clamp' and 'the missing object shrinks to ONE finite cell' are overclaims: all-recurrent-SCC imprimitivity is a universally quantified invariant over the entire 5-coloring solution space, not obviously weaker than one forced pair, and NO abstract (geometry-free) imprimitive 5-color cell has been exhibited at all. The analogue of the 48-vertex abstract-clamp milestone is missing entirely; the proposal trades one missing object for another with zero existence evidence at any level, against the known entropy headwind (5-coloring richness manufactures coprime short cycles, the same force that killed forcing in L45/L56/L57).

Framing slips in first_principles: the plane's unit-distance graph is not an orbit equivalence relation of a countable group (every vertex has continuum many neighbors), so 'Isom(R^2) amenable => hyperfinite => spectrum-vs-holonomy is the ONLY possible mechanism' is a heuristic presented as an exclusivity theorem. Does not damage Part A but the 'MUST' claim is unproven.

Arithmetic precision gap in C3: 'spindling angles are automatically irrational multiples of pi' has exactly four exceptional carrier radii on the cocircular family (rho in {1/2, sqrt(1/3), sqrt(1/2), 1} give alpha in {pi, 2pi/3, pi/2, pi/3}); the censorship conjecture must exclude these explicitly, and C2(b)'s thresholds (>= 7 cocircular vertices, no width 2-3 cell) plus C1 Part B's |F| <= 200 are numerology with no derivation (L55 only kills width 1).

C4 as a universal claim is likely false in generality (its own caveat is right: S_5 coset alignment and within-class entropy can produce continuum overlaps even when the phase invariant is real); it is salvageable only as a per-cell computational probe, not as a theorem-shaped conjecture.
prior_art_collisions: Kechris-Solecki-Todorcevic: the eigenfunction obstruction IS the classical mechanism behind chi_B = 3 for the irrational rotation graph; SHC's baby case (one carrier vertex, k = 2, T = 2-cycle) literally reproduces it, and k = 3 correctly goes silent. The mechanism is classical; only the finite-cell transfer-digraph packaging for unit-distance graphs is new.

Katznelson 2001 (chromatic numbers of Cayley graphs on Z and recurrence): measurable colorings versus rotation/Bohr spectra on distance graphs; nearest analytic prior art for 'a coloring forbids a rotation eigenvalue'.

Gao-Jackson-Krohne-Seward continuous combinatorics of Z^d shifts: continuous/Borel chromatic numbers exceeding chi via phase obstructions over hyperfinite dynamics; same spectrum-vs-holonomy phenomenon, never on unit-distance graphs of the plane.
Laczkovich circle-squaring and Conley-Grebik-Pikhurko measurable tilings: holonomy obstructions over amenable dynamics, cited by the proposal; legitimate adjacency, no overlap on chromatic numbers.

Repo L55 wildcard B already introduced transfer-matrix imprimitivity as the correct diagnostic (and the monoid obstruction proves width-1 interfaces are always primitive, which the proposal correctly respects via width >= 2); the genuinely new ingredient relative to the repo is replacing finite closure with the irrational-rotation eigenvalue argument. No eigenfunction/Koopman/irrational-rotation content exists anywhere in experiments/ (grep verified).

Atlas audit (docs/research_atlas/arch2_measurable_lineage.md line 56): no chi_m(R^2) >= 6 is published as of May 2026; the literature's 'chi_m >= 6' results are hyperbolic-plane misattributions (DeCorte-Golubev 2018). So C1's conditional conclusion chi_m >= 6 would be genuinely new; note CLAUDE.md's 'chi_m >= 6 in recent work' is exactly the misattribution the atlas audited.

Exoo-Ismailescu and Polymath16 spindle-chains around circles: aimed at exact algebraic closure; the rational-angle special case of SHC (closed N-cell annulus with no recurrent period dividing N gives a finite chi >= 6 UDG) strictly subsumes that finite route, which is a point in the proposal's favor, not against it.

Marks determinacy correctly excluded: it requires nonamenable (free-product) group actions, unavailable over Isom(R^2); the proposal's use of this exclusion is right even though its hyperfiniteness framing is loose.
result.surviving_kernel: C1 Part A survives hostile re-derivation as a conditional theorem: if a finite UDG cell F and unit-preserving rotation R_alpha (alpha/(2 pi) irrational) have a 5-color transfer digraph in which EVERY recurrent SCC has period >= 2, then no Lebesgue-measurable proper 5-coloring of R^2 exists (chi_m >= 6, chi_B >= 6). Every step checks out: Fubini offset for measurability of the section, bounded height function a.e.-invariant under the measure-preserving rotation, arcs between distinct SCCs strictly drop height so the walk is a.e. confined to one SCC, cyclic classes yield a measurable eigenfunction with a primitive p-th root of unity eigenvalue, impossible for an ergodic irrational rotation whose eigenvalue group is {e^(i n alpha)}. Equally real: F union R(F) is AUTOMATICALLY unit-distance realizable, so W3's exact-closure wall is genuinely deleted from the hypothesis, and the rational-angle special case correctly yields a finite chi >= 6 UDG (closed annulus, no recurrent period dividing N), giving the search a two-way payoff that unifies the finite and measurable routes. The detectors pass for structural reasons (no rotation dynamics in L^infty, no invariant probability on R^1, no invariant measure charging Q^2), repairing the exact L^infty failure that sank the L54 borel world. What does NOT survive is any evidence that Part B's object exists: the entire program now hangs on a single open combinatorial existence question (an all-recurrent-SCCs-imprimitive 5-color cell), which may die the clamp's death.
result.cheapest_decisive_test: Skip geometry entirely. Stage 0, abstract existence: enumerate small abstract cells (graph F on <= 8-10 vertices, arbitrary cross relation X on V(F) with loops allowed), build the 5-color transfer digraph projected to interface vertices (<= 5^8 states; python-sat for coloring enumeration, networkx for SCCs and gcd-of-cycle-length periods), and ask whether ANY cell has EVERY recurrent SCC with period >= 2. Hours of pure Python on this host, no new infrastructure. Outcome (a): a hit proves the abstract object exists and the fight becomes geometric realization on the alpha-stable cocircular carrier family (then run the sympy minimal_polynomial(2*cos(alpha)) test to learn which side of the rational/irrational dichotomy fires). Outcome (b): an exhaustive small-cell all-primitive result, especially if accompanied by an L55-style symmetry argument, presumptively kills Part B the way the monoid killed width-1 forcing. Built-in soundness control: run the identical code at k = 7; Part A plus the Borel hex 7-coloring implies no all-imprimitive 7-color cell can exist, so any k = 7 hit exposes a code bug or refutes Part A.
result.steelman: Restate SHC on the interface-projected transfer digraph (sound and computable), with the promotion gate corrected to: every recurrent SCC imprimitive. Stage the program as: (0) decide abstract existence of an all-imprimitive 5-color cell on small cells, with the k = 7 run as soundness control; (1) if abstract cells exist, sweep ONLY alpha-stable families (cocircular carrier, cos alpha = 1 - 1/(2 rho^2), cross-edge set verified constant on a rho-interval, alpha-irrationality certified per cell by sympy), treating lineage slicings purely as primitivity calibration with no claimed C1 transfer; (2) exploit the two-way payoff: a hit at one of the four exceptional radii (rho in {1/2, sqrt(1/3), sqrt(1/2), 1}) closes a finite annulus and yields a finite chi >= 6 UDG, while a hit at any other radius fires the measurable/Borel bound chi_m >= 6, chi_B >= 6, the first such bound in print and the concrete form of L54's dark horse; (3) hand Part A to VERIFIER/Lean as three named lemmas (Fubini offset measurability, height a.e.-invariance under a measure-preserving ergodic rotation, eigenvalue group of an ergodic rotation contains no root of unity except 1). Drop the 'strictly less than the clamp' rhetoric: this is a lateral trade that deletes the W3 closure wall at the price of a new existence question, and its honest value is that the new question is SAT-searchable today on this repo's stack, calibrates correctly on every known control, and pays out in both the finite and the definable world.
